name: Fuente Obejuna — circular by the Río Guadiato
name_original: Fuente Obejuna — circular por el río Guadiato
region_intro: Hiking trails around Fuente Obejuna traverse the Guadiato Valley in the north of Córdoba, characterized by Mediterranean forest landscapes and dehesas. The region features gentle hills and extensive plains, with a mosaic of oak and cork oak pastures. Key natural features include the Río Guadiato and the Sierra Boyera Reservoir, offering varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Fuente Obejuna The most popular hiking route is Fuente Obejuna — circular by the Río Guadiato, an 8.7 miles (14.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Guadiato River and offers panoramic views of Fuente Obejuna. Another top favourite among local hikers is Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This circular route provides views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir and allows hikers to traverse its dam. Local hikers also love the Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Fuente Obejuna is defined by dehesas, river valleys, and reservoir surroundings. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to moderate longer paths. The routes in Fuente Obejuna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuente Obejuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Fuente Obejuna offers a diverse network of around 12 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the region's Mediterranean forest landscapes, dehesas, and river valleys.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, Fuente Obejuna has several easy hiking options. Approximately half of the trails are classified as easy, perfect for a relaxed stroll or a family outing. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199599630/el-zurquillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Zurquillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter route that can be completed in just over an hour.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Hikers in Fuente Obejuna will experience the characteristic landscapes of the Guadiato Valley, including picturesque 'dehesas' with oak and cork oak pastures, gentle hills, and extensive plains. The region also features river valleys, such as along the Río Guadiato, and the scenic surroundings of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Fuente Obejuna are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249053303/belmez-and-sierra-boyera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views and allows you to traverse the dam.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Along the trails, you can discover the tranquil course of the Río Guadiato, the expansive Sierra Boyera Reservoir, and the unique dehesa landscapes. You might also encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself, which offers a glimpse into the local culture.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The trails in Fuente Obejuna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to reservoir surroundings and expansive dehesas, and the opportunities to immerse themselves in the rural beauty of the Cordoban countryside.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249099926/fuente-obejuna-circular-by-the-rio-guadiato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna — circular by the Río Guadiato&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers a panoramic view of Fuente Obejuna from a viewpoint along the way, in addition to following the scenic Guadiato River.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Hikes in Fuente Obejuna vary in length and duration. Shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199599630/el-zurquillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Zurquillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248986408/belmez-from-penarroya-pueblonuevo-circular-by-the-via-verde-del-guadiato-and-los-pedroches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours and 45 minutes.

Q: Is Fuente Obejuna a good destination for wildlife spotting?
A: The dehesa landscapes and Mediterranean forests of Fuente Obejuna are home to diverse wildlife, including the famous Iberian pig. While specific wildlife spotting trails are not highlighted, the natural environment provides opportunities to observe local flora and fauna, especially in quieter areas along the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Given the region's long, dry summers, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for hiking in Fuente Obejuna. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be cooler. Summer hikes are possible but best undertaken early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any specific cultural or historical routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region features routes like the Ruta de los Pilares, a medium-difficulty lineal route connecting hamlets and offering ethnographical, natural, historical, and scenic interest. Additionally, the Rutas de la Encomienda Mellariense are a series of trails that connect various hamlets, providing a deeper dive into the local heritage.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other outdoor activities in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Absolutely. Beyond hiking, Fuente Obejuna's varied landscapes are also well-suited for other outdoor pursuits such as cycling, mountain biking, and road cycling. The network of trails and rural roads provides ample opportunities for multi-sport adventures.
tips:
This town is perhaps best known for inspiring Lope de Vega's work "Fuenteovejuna" about the events that took place here in the 15th century that ended with the death of the village commander Fernán Gómez de Guzmán at the hands of the residents.

It has also often been associated with the Roman municipality of Mellaria, although the most recent historical investigations make it clear that this Roman town does not share the same past as Fuente Obejuna and that it was actually located on a nearby hill.
On the way to the Guadito river and El Hoyo you get a panoramic view of the town of Fuente Obejuna.
Hiking route along the Guadiato River when it runs between two hills near El Hoya. There are some ruins of an old water mill used to grind flour.

waypoints.name: Molino del Hoyo on the Guadiato River
